title: momordica cabrae
intro: Momordica cabrae, also known as the West African bitter apple or bitter cucumber, is a plant species within the Cucurbitaceae family, native to tropical Africa. The plant is used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antipyretic properties. The health benefits of Momordica cabrae include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and aid in the treatment of digestive issues, such as diarrhea and constipation. The herbal preparations of Momordica cabrae can be made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, which are often used in traditional medicine to treat a range of health conditions.
